{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents = [[Takamimusubi]] | image = File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg | siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Kuebiko]] * [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database
{{jmyth navbox long}}

[[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Japanese gods]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Wisdom gods]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Shinto kami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]][[en:Omoikane]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Omoikane]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] {{Japan-myth-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents = [[Takamimusubi]] | image = File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg | siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Kuebiko]] * [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database
{{jmyth navbox long}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents = [[Takamimusubi]] | image = File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg | siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Kuebiko]] * [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database
{{jmyth navbox long}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents = [[Takamimusubi]] | image = File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg | siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Kuebiko]] * [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database
{{jmyth navbox long}}

revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents = [[Takamimusubi]] | image = File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg | siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * [[Kuebiko]] * [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database
{{jmyth navbox long}}

'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).

==Shrines== 
*Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture
*Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture
*Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture

{{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| name = Omoikane
| god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence
| parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]]
| gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}
| offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723}}
| deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799}}
| equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}

'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.

He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦). 

He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.

However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).

==Shrines== 
*Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture
*Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture
*Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture

==See also==
* [[Amaterasu]]
* [[Benzaiten]]
* [[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]]
* [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]

He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q586088]] Wikidata name: Omoikane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[en:Omoikane]]<!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[fr:Omoikane]] [[ja:オモイカネ]] [[ka:ომოიკანე]] [[ko:오모이카네]] [[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]] [[ru:Омойканэ]] [[sv:Omoigane]] [[zh:思兼神]] {{wikidata link|Q586088}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q586088]] Wikidata name: Omoikane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * [[:en:Kuebiko|Kuebiko]] * [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database

He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture

'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''[[:en:Ame-no-oshihomimi|Ame-no-Oshihomimi]]'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in [[:en:Chichibu Province|Chichibu Province]] (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]]. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q586088]] Wikidata name: Omoikane [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi}}'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * {{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko}} * {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database

In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi}}'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * {{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko}} * {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}
==References== {{reflist}}

{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi}}'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * {{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko}} * {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omoikane]][[fr:Omoikane]][[ja:オモイカネ]][[ka:ომოიკანე]][[ko:오모이카네]][[mni:ꯑꯣꯃꯣꯢꯀꯅꯦ (ꯁꯤꯟꯇꯣ)]][[ru:Омойканэ]][[sv:Omoigane]][[zh:思兼神]]
{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723|1=Uwaharu and Shitaharu}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799|1=Wisdom}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province|1=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * {{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko|1=Kuebiko}} * {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125 Omoikane], Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/ Omoikane] - History of Japan Database

'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).

However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province|1=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. '''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.

He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦). 

He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.

However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province|1=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).

==Shrines== 
*Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture
*Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture
*Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture

{{short description|Shinto god of wisdom and intelligence}} {{refimprove|date=March 2017}} {{disputed|date=March 2017}} {{For|the anime containing a fictional computer with a similar name|Martian Successor Nadesico}}{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | name = Omoikane | god_of = God of Wisdom and Intelligence | parents =[[Takamimusubi]]; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}}| image =File:Omoikane drawn by William Aston.jpg; File:Omoikane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| siblings = [[Futodama]] [[Takuhadachiji-hime]] | gender = {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | offspring = {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|WD=Q10940723|1=Uwaharu and Shitaharu}} | deity_of = Kami of {{ill|wisdom|WD=Q799|1=Wisdom}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} }}
'''Omoikane''' (思兼 or 思金) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami]] of wisdom and intelligence. His name means "having the wisdom and thoughtfulness of many people".<ref>{{cite book |last1=川口 |first1=謙二 |title=日本の神様読み解き事典 |date=2012 |publisher=柏書房}}</ref> A heavenly deity who is called upon to "ponder" and give good counsel in the deliberations of the heavenly deities. In the myth where [[Amaterasu]] hid in a cave, he was entrusted with the task of finding a way to get her out.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Omoikane • A History of Japan - 日本歴史|url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/omoikane/|access-date=2020-10-11|website=A History of Japan - 日本歴史|language=en-GB}}</ref> Carpenters pray to him during the construction of pillars in a building. Usually, Japanese people pray to Omoikane for success in school and exams.
He is known by other names as ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神) in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (古事記); ''Omoikane'' (思兼神) in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (日本書紀); ''Omokane'' (思金神, 思兼神), ''Tokoyo-no-Omoikane'' (常世思金神), ''Yagokoro-omoikane'' (八意思兼神, 八意思金神) in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀), or ''Achihiko'' (阿智彦).
He is the son of creator deity [[Takamimusubi]]<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Omoikane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=125|access-date=2020-12-11|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref> (高御産巣日神) and the older brother of ''Takuhatachiji-hime'' (栲幡千千姫命, or commonly named in the ''[[Kojiki]]'': 万幡豊秋津師比売命 ''Yorozuhatatoyo'akitsushi-hime''), who is married to the deity ''{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-Oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}'' (天忍穂耳命). In the [[Kojiki]] [[Tenson kōrin]] myth, Omoikane is one of the ''kami'' who descends to earth.
However, in the ''[[Kujiki]]'' (旧事紀 or ''Sendai Kyuji Hongi'' 先代旧事本紀). Omoikane descends to {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (信濃国 ''Shinano-no-kuni'', a former province that is now {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) to become the ancestor of ''Shina-no-achihouri'' (信之阿智祝) and as in {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|WD=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province|1=Chichibu Province}} (知々夫国, ''Chichibu no kuni''), a former province in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}. He then becomes the father of both deities, ''Ame-no-Uwaharu'' (天表春命) and ''Ame-no-Shitaharu'' (天下春命), also through this lineage becomes the patriarchal ancestor of the children of ''[[Ame-no-Koyane|Ama-no-Koyane]]'' (天児屋命, 天児屋根命).
==Shrines== *Achi shrine, Kurashiki, Okayama prefecture *Chichibu shrine, Chichibu, Saitama prefecture *Shizu shrine, Naka, Ibaraki prefecture
==See also== * [[Amaterasu]] * [[Benzaiten]] * {{ill|Kuebiko|en|Kuebiko|WD=Q6441847|lt=Kuebiko|1=Kuebiko}} * {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}
